title: dianthus seguieri subsp. collinus
intro: Dianthus seguieri subsp. collinus, also known as the pyrenean thrift, is a perennial plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the mountains of the Pyrenees. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and antiseptic qualities, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dianthus seguieri subsp. collinus include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, teas, and ointments, and it can be combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
